findings.title: Custom slab allocator reversed and heap state confirmed but safe-linking blocks fake object injection
findings.statement: The heap-analysis skill guided construction of a GDB Python walker for both the thread-local cache tier and the central arena. Heap state after a controlled free sequence is deterministic: slot reuse follows a LIFO pattern in the thread-local cache. However, the allocator encodes free-list pointers using safe-linking (XOR with a per-slab random key stored at an unreadable offset). Without a separate leak to recover the XOR key, fake object placement fails the integrity check on re-allocation and the process aborts.

evidence.summary: debug_gdb_script: tier-specific Python walker dumps thread-local cache free-list and central arena state
debug_gdb_commands: breakpoint on re-allocation confirms LIFO slot reuse in thread-local cache tier
debug_gdb_commands: safe-linking integrity check fails on injected pointer — XOR key unrecoverable without separate leak
